summary: Extraction fidelity is acceptable for this registry text-snapshot normalization. The section index contains eight stable sections covering trial overview, study design, conditions, arms/interventions, eligibility, protocol outcomes, posted outcome measures, and adverse events. The normalized markdown preserves registry module locators and key posted-result group/analysis values, while explicitly limiting itself to a ClinicalTrials.gov registry record rather than article full text.

findings.claim_or_issue: The text snapshot has eight sections, matching the generated sections.json module index.
findings.why_it_matters: Stable section IDs and source locators let future agents cite reusable registry text regions rather than rereading the full raw JSON.
findings.recommended_action: Use section IDs such as posted-results-outcomes and adverse-events in future registry extraction-refresh provenance.

findings.claim_or_issue: The normalized posted-results section preserves outcome titles, time frames, group labels, measured values, and p-values for the key registry outcome measures.
findings.why_it_matters: This makes the markdown useful for extraction agents while raw JSON remains available for audit and parser repair.
findings.recommended_action: Route any future result-record repair through the retained raw JSON and section-index artifacts before updating canonical results.

findings.claim_or_issue: The text_snapshot quality limitations state that this is normalized ClinicalTrials.gov registry text, not article full text, and that markdown is derived from selected registry modules.
findings.why_it_matters: Consumers and agents should not treat this artifact as a complete publication or supplementary-material extraction.
findings.recommended_action: Keep full publication/table extraction as a separate source-rights and text-snapshot path.
